fields.title: Clean Code
fields.author: Robert C. Martin
fields.description: A practical guide to writing readable, maintainable, and high quality software. The book teaches principles, patterns, and best practices for clean programming through real code examples, showing how developers can improve code structure, naming, testing, and design.

fields.title: The Pragmatic Programmer
fields.author: Andrew Hunt
fields.description: A practical guide for software developers that emphasizes craftsmanship, problem solving, and adaptable thinking. Through tips and real examples, the book teaches best practices for coding, debugging, testing, and building reliable and maintainable software.

fields.title: Introduction to Algorithms
fields.author: Thomas H. Cormen
fields.description: A widely used textbook that presents a comprehensive introduction to algorithms and data structures. It covers algorithm design techniques, sorting, graphs, dynamic programming, and computational complexity with rigorous explanations and detailed analysis.

fields.title: Design Patterns
fields.author: Erich Gamma
fields.description: A classic software engineering book that introduces reusable solutions to common object oriented design problems. It presents 23 design patterns including Singleton, Factory, Observer, and Strategy to help developers build flexible, maintainable software systems.

fields.title: Deep Learning
fields.author: Ian Goodfellow
fields.description: A comprehensive textbook on deep learning that explains the mathematical foundations, neural network architectures, optimization techniques, and practical applications of modern machine learning. It covers topics such as CNNs, RNNs, regularization, and representation learning.

fields.title: Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach
fields.author: Stuart Russell
fields.description: A foundational textbook on artificial intelligence covering search, knowledge representation, reasoning, machine learning, natural language processing, robotics, and ethics. It presents the core concepts and algorithms that underpin modern AI systems and intelligent agents.

fields.title: Computer Networks
fields.author: Andrew S. Tanenbaum
fields.description: A comprehensive introduction to computer networking covering the architecture and protocols of modern networks. The book explains the physical layer to application layer, including Ethernet, Wi Fi, routing, congestion control, and Internet protocols with real world examples.

fields.title: Operating System Concepts
fields.author: Abraham Silberschatz
fields.description: A widely used textbook that explains the principles of modern operating systems. It covers processes, threads, CPU scheduling, memory management, file systems, virtualization, and security, providing both theoretical foundations and practical examples.

fields.title: Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs
fields.author: Harold Abelson
fields.description: A classic computer science textbook that teaches fundamental programming concepts through abstraction, recursion, interpreters, and language design. Using Scheme, it shows how complex systems can be built from simple ideas and emphasizes deep understanding of computation.

fields.title: Code Complete
fields.author: Steve McConnell
fields.description: A comprehensive guide to software construction that covers coding techniques, design practices, debugging, testing, and maintainability. It emphasizes writing clear, reliable code and provides practical advice to help developers build high quality software systems.

fields.title: Refactoring
fields.author: Martin Fowler
fields.description: A practical guide to improving existing code without changing its behavior. The book introduces systematic refactoring techniques that help developers restructure code, remove duplication, improve readability, and maintain long term software quality.

fields.title: The Mythical Man Month
fields.author: Fred Brooks
fields.description: A classic book on software project management that explains why adding more programmers to a late project often makes it later. Through essays and real experiences, it explores planning, communication, complexity, and the challenges of large software development.

fields.title: Head First Design Patterns
fields.author: Eric Freeman
fields.description: An engaging introduction to software design patterns that teaches reusable solutions to common programming problems. Using visual explanations and practical examples, the book explains patterns such as Observer, Factory, Singleton, and Strategy in an easy to understand way.

fields.title: Programming Pearls
fields.author: Jon Bentley
fields.description: A classic programming book that presents elegant solutions to real programming problems. Through practical examples and problem solving techniques, it teaches algorithm design, performance thinking, and how to write efficient, clear, and thoughtful code.

fields.title: Algorithms
fields.author: Robert Sedgewick
fields.description: A comprehensive introduction to algorithms and data structures. The book explains sorting, searching, graphs, strings, and optimization techniques with clear explanations and Java implementations, helping readers understand both algorithm design and practical performance analysis.

fields.title: Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning
fields.author: Christopher Bishop
fields.description: A rigorous introduction to machine learning from a probabilistic perspective. The book covers classification, regression, neural networks, graphical models, kernel methods, and Bayesian inference, providing strong mathematical foundations for pattern recognition.

fields.title: Hands On Machine Learning
fields.author: Aurelien Geron
fields.description: A practical guide to machine learning using Python. The book walks through real examples using Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow to build models for classification, regression, neural networks, and deep learning, focusing on hands-on implementation and best practices.

fields.title: The Art of Computer Programming
fields.author: Donald Knuth
fields.description: A legendary series that explores algorithms and programming techniques in depth. It covers fundamental algorithms, data structures, combinatorial methods, and mathematical analysis, providing rigorous insights into the theory and practice of computer programming.

fields.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
fields.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
fields.description: A collection of twelve detective stories featuring Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson. Through clever deduction and observation, Holmes solves mysterious crimes across Victorian London, showcasing his brilliant analytical mind and unique investigative methods.

fields.title: Pride and Prejudice
fields.author: Jane Austen
fields.description: Elizabeth Bennet navigates family expectations, social class, and misunderstandings while forming a complicated relationship with the reserved Mr. Darcy. The novel explores love, pride, prejudice, and personal growth in early nineteenth century English society.

fields.author: George Orwell
fields.description: 1984 is a dystopian novel set in a totalitarian society ruled by constant surveillance and propaganda. Winston Smith struggles to preserve truth and individuality while living under the oppressive control of Big Brother and the Party.

fields.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
fields.author: Harper Lee
fields.description: Told through the eyes of young Scout Finch, the story follows her father Atticus, a lawyer defending a Black man falsely accused of a crime in the American South. The novel explores racial injustice, moral courage, empathy, and the loss of childhood innocence.

fields.title: The Great Gatsby
fields.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
fields.description: Set in the Roaring Twenties, the story follows Nick Carraway as he becomes fascinated by his wealthy neighbor Jay Gatsby, who throws lavish parties while longing for his lost love Daisy. The novel explores wealth, illusion, ambition, and the American Dream.

fields.title: Moby Dick
fields.author: Herman Melville
fields.description: Narrated by Ishmael, the novel follows Captain Ahab�s obsessive quest to hunt the legendary white whale, Moby Dick. The voyage of the whaling ship Pequod becomes a powerful exploration of obsession, fate, nature, and humanity�s struggle against the unknown.

fields.title: War and Peace
fields.author: Leo Tolstoy
fields.description: An epic historical novel that follows several Russian families during the Napoleonic Wars. Through characters such as Pierre Bezukhov, Prince Andrei, and Natasha Rostova, the story explores war, love, destiny, society, and the search for meaning in life.

fields.title: Crime and Punishment
fields.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
fields.description: The story follows Raskolnikov, a poor student in St. Petersburg who commits a murder believing he is intellectually superior to moral law. As guilt and paranoia consume him, the novel explores conscience, redemption, justice, and the psychological burden of crime.

fields.title: The Catcher in the Rye
fields.author: J. D. Salinger
fields.description: The novel follows Holden Caulfield, a disillusioned teenager who leaves his prep school and wanders through New York City. Through his reflections and encounters, the story explores alienation, identity, innocence, and the struggles of growing up.

fields.title: Brave New World
fields.author: Aldous Huxley
fields.description: Set in a futuristic society where humans are genetically engineered and conditioned for stability and happiness, the novel follows Bernard Marx and John the Savage as they confront a world that sacrifices individuality and freedom for comfort and control.

fields.title: The Hobbit
fields.author: J. R. R. Tolkien
fields.description: Bilbo Baggins, a quiet hobbit, is unexpectedly drawn into an adventure with a group of dwarves and the wizard Gandalf. Their quest to reclaim a stolen treasure from the dragon Smaug becomes a journey of courage, discovery, and transformation.

fields.title: The Lord of the Rings
fields.author: J. R. R. Tolkien
fields.description: Frodo Baggins and a fellowship of allies embark on a dangerous quest to destroy the One Ring and defeat the dark lord Sauron. Their journey across Middle-earth explores friendship, courage, sacrifice, and the struggle between good and evil.

fields.title: Jane Eyre
fields.author: Charlotte Bronte
fields.description: Jane Eyre, an orphan raised in hardship, grows into an independent and strong willed woman. Working as a governess at Thornfield Hall, she develops a complicated relationship with the mysterious Mr. Rochester while seeking love, dignity, and self respect.

fields.title: Wuthering Heights
fields.author: Emily Bronte
fields.description: A dark and passionate tale set on the Yorkshire moors, the novel follows the intense and destructive relationship between Heathcliff and Catherine Earnshaw. Themes of love, revenge, obsession, and social class drive this haunting story.

fields.title: Dracula
fields.author: Bram Stoker
fields.description: A Gothic horror novel that follows Jonathan Harker and a group of allies as they confront the mysterious Count Dracula. Told through letters and journal entries, the story explores fear, superstition, and the battle between good and evil.

fields.title: Frankenstein
fields.author: Mary Shelley
fields.description: Victor Frankenstein, a young scientist, creates a living being through an experimental scientific process. When the creature is rejected by its creator and society, tragedy unfolds. The novel explores ambition, responsibility, loneliness, and the ethics of scientific discovery.

fields.title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
fields.author: Oscar Wilde
fields.description: Dorian Gray remains eternally young while a hidden portrait of him ages and reflects the corruption of his soul. As he pursues pleasure and vanity, the novel explores beauty, morality, influence, and the consequences of a life without conscience.

fields.title: Great Expectations
fields.author: Charles Dickens
fields.description: The story follows Pip, an orphan who receives an unexpected fortune and moves to London believing it will help him become a gentleman worthy of Estella. Through his journey, Pip learns hard lessons about wealth, loyalty, love, and personal growth.

fields.title: A Tale of Two Cities
fields.author: Charles Dickens
fields.description: Set during the French Revolution, the novel follows Charles Darnay and Sydney Carton as their lives become intertwined with Lucie Manette in London and Paris. It explores sacrifice, resurrection, injustice, and the human cost of revolution.

fields.title: The Alchemist
fields.author: Paulo Coelho
fields.description: A young shepherd named Santiago travels from Spain to the Egyptian desert in search of a hidden treasure after having a recurring dream. Along the journey he learns about destiny, listening to the heart, and pursuing one's Personal Legend.

fields.title: Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs
fields.author: Harold Abelson
fields.description: A classic computer science textbook that teaches fundamental programming concepts through abstraction, recursion, interpreters, and language design. Using Scheme, it shows how complex systems can be built from simple ideas and emphasizes deep understanding of computation.

fields.title: The Great Gatsby
fields.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
fields.description: Set in the Roaring Twenties, the story follows Nick Carraway as he becomes fascinated by his wealthy neighbor Jay Gatsby, who throws lavish parties while longing for his lost love Daisy. The novel explores wealth, illusion, ambition, and the American Dream.

fields.title: Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach
fields.author: Stuart Russell
fields.description: A foundational textbook on artificial intelligence covering search, knowledge representation, reasoning, machine learning, natural language processing, robotics, and ethics. It presents the core concepts and algorithms that underpin modern AI systems and intelligent agents.

fields.title: Wuthering Heights
fields.author: Emily Bronte
fields.description: A dark and passionate tale set on the Yorkshire moors, the novel follows the intense and destructive relationship between Heathcliff and Catherine Earnshaw. Themes of love, revenge, obsession, and social class drive this haunting story.

fields.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
fields.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
fields.description: A collection of twelve detective stories featuring Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson. Through clever deduction and observation, Holmes solves mysterious crimes across Victorian London, showcasing his brilliant analytical mind and unique investigative methods.

fields.title: Dracula
fields.author: Bram Stoker
fields.description: A Gothic horror novel that follows Jonathan Harker and a group of allies as they confront the mysterious Count Dracula. Told through letters and journal entries, the story explores fear, superstition, and the battle between good and evil.

fields.title: Wuthering Heights
fields.author: Emily Bronte
fields.description: A dark and passionate tale set on the Yorkshire moors, the novel follows the intense and destructive relationship between Heathcliff and Catherine Earnshaw. Themes of love, revenge, obsession, and social class drive this haunting story.

fields.title: Pride and Prejudice
fields.author: Jane Austen
fields.description: Elizabeth Bennet navigates family expectations, social class, and misunderstandings while forming a complicated relationship with the reserved Mr. Darcy. The novel explores love, pride, prejudice, and personal growth in early nineteenth century English society.

fields.author: George Orwell
fields.description: 1984 is a dystopian novel set in a totalitarian society ruled by constant surveillance and propaganda. Winston Smith struggles to preserve truth and individuality while living under the oppressive control of Big Brother and the Party.

fields.title: Pride and Prejudice
fields.author: Jane Austen
fields.description: Elizabeth Bennet navigates family expectations, social class, and misunderstandings while forming a complicated relationship with the reserved Mr. Darcy. The novel explores love, pride, prejudice, and personal growth in early nineteenth century English society.

fields.title: A Tale of Two Cities
fields.author: Charles Dickens
fields.description: Set during the French Revolution, the novel follows Charles Darnay and Sydney Carton as their lives become intertwined with Lucie Manette in London and Paris. It explores sacrifice, resurrection, injustice, and the human cost of revolution.

fields.title: Design Patterns
fields.author: Erich Gamma
fields.description: A classic software engineering book that introduces reusable solutions to common object oriented design problems. It presents 23 design patterns including Singleton, Factory, Observer, and Strategy to help developers build flexible, maintainable software systems.

fields.title: Dracula
fields.author: Bram Stoker
fields.description: A Gothic horror novel that follows Jonathan Harker and a group of allies as they confront the mysterious Count Dracula. Told through letters and journal entries, the story explores fear, superstition, and the battle between good and evil.

fields.title: Algorithms
fields.author: Robert Sedgewick
fields.description: A comprehensive introduction to algorithms and data structures. The book explains sorting, searching, graphs, strings, and optimization techniques with clear explanations and Java implementations, helping readers understand both algorithm design and practical performance analysis.

fields.title: Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs
fields.author: Harold Abelson
fields.description: A classic computer science textbook that teaches fundamental programming concepts through abstraction, recursion, interpreters, and language design. Using Scheme, it shows how complex systems can be built from simple ideas and emphasizes deep understanding of computation.

fields.title: Crime and Punishment
fields.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
fields.description: The story follows Raskolnikov, a poor student in St. Petersburg who commits a murder believing he is intellectually superior to moral law. As guilt and paranoia consume him, the novel explores conscience, redemption, justice, and the psychological burden of crime.

fields.title: Code Complete
fields.author: Steve McConnell
fields.description: A comprehensive guide to software construction that covers coding techniques, design practices, debugging, testing, and maintainability. It emphasizes writing clear, reliable code and provides practical advice to help developers build high quality software systems.

fields.title: The Mythical Man Month
fields.author: Fred Brooks
fields.description: A classic book on software project management that explains why adding more programmers to a late project often makes it later. Through essays and real experiences, it explores planning, communication, complexity, and the challenges of large software development.

fields.title: Head First Design Patterns
fields.author: Eric Freeman
fields.description: An engaging introduction to software design patterns that teaches reusable solutions to common programming problems. Using visual explanations and practical examples, the book explains patterns such as Observer, Factory, Singleton, and Strategy in an easy to understand way.

fields.title: Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning
fields.author: Christopher Bishop
fields.description: A rigorous introduction to machine learning from a probabilistic perspective. The book covers classification, regression, neural networks, graphical models, kernel methods, and Bayesian inference, providing strong mathematical foundations for pattern recognition.

fields.title: Operating System Concepts
fields.author: Abraham Silberschatz
fields.description: A widely used textbook that explains the principles of modern operating systems. It covers processes, threads, CPU scheduling, memory management, file systems, virtualization, and security, providing both theoretical foundations and practical examples.

fields.title: Refactoring
fields.author: Martin Fowler
fields.description: A practical guide to improving existing code without changing its behavior. The book introduces systematic refactoring techniques that help developers restructure code, remove duplication, improve readability, and maintain long term software quality.

fields.title: The Pragmatic Programmer
fields.author: Andrew Hunt
fields.description: A practical guide for software developers that emphasizes craftsmanship, problem solving, and adaptable thinking. Through tips and real examples, the book teaches best practices for coding, debugging, testing, and building reliable and maintainable software.

fields.title: The Hobbit
fields.author: J. R. R. Tolkien
fields.description: Bilbo Baggins, a quiet hobbit, is unexpectedly drawn into an adventure with a group of dwarves and the wizard Gandalf. Their quest to reclaim a stolen treasure from the dragon Smaug becomes a journey of courage, discovery, and transformation.

fields.title: The Alchemist
fields.author: Paulo Coelho
fields.description: A young shepherd named Santiago travels from Spain to the Egyptian desert in search of a hidden treasure after having a recurring dream. Along the journey he learns about destiny, listening to the heart, and pursuing one's Personal Legend.

fields.author: George Orwell
fields.description: 1984 is a dystopian novel set in a totalitarian society ruled by constant surveillance and propaganda. Winston Smith struggles to preserve truth and individuality while living under the oppressive control of Big Brother and the Party.

fields.title: Great Expectations
fields.author: Charles Dickens
fields.description: The story follows Pip, an orphan who receives an unexpected fortune and moves to London believing it will help him become a gentleman worthy of Estella. Through his journey, Pip learns hard lessons about wealth, loyalty, love, and personal growth.

fields.title: Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning
fields.author: Christopher Bishop
fields.description: A rigorous introduction to machine learning from a probabilistic perspective. The book covers classification, regression, neural networks, graphical models, kernel methods, and Bayesian inference, providing strong mathematical foundations for pattern recognition.

fields.title: The Lord of the Rings
fields.author: J. R. R. Tolkien
fields.description: Frodo Baggins and a fellowship of allies embark on a dangerous quest to destroy the One Ring and defeat the dark lord Sauron. Their journey across Middle-earth explores friendship, courage, sacrifice, and the struggle between good and evil.

fields.title: Great Expectations
fields.author: Charles Dickens
fields.description: The story follows Pip, an orphan who receives an unexpected fortune and moves to London believing it will help him become a gentleman worthy of Estella. Through his journey, Pip learns hard lessons about wealth, loyalty, love, and personal growth.

fields.title: The Catcher in the Rye
fields.author: J. D. Salinger
fields.description: The novel follows Holden Caulfield, a disillusioned teenager who leaves his prep school and wanders through New York City. Through his reflections and encounters, the story explores alienation, identity, innocence, and the struggles of growing up.

fields.author: Charles Dickens
